repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Congregational Church of West Groton records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Congregational Church of West Groton (West Groton, N.Y.)

did.abstract: Two volumes containing membership lists, monthly meeting minutes (1817-1874), and trustee's annual meeting minutes (1832-1900).

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The Congregational church of West Groton was formed at the home of Ichabod Brown by Reverends Joshua Dean and Seth Smith as the West Church of Locke in 1816. The name of the church was changed to the Congregational Church of West Groton in 1818. The church was organized into a society in 1832 under the "Plan of Union." In 1850 the Congregational churches in New York State began to withdraw from the Plan. In 1866 the West Groton church heeded a call from the Homer church to withdraw. A church building was erected in 1833 and the Sunday School was started in 1848.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The records of the West Groton Congregational Church contain a covenant meeting minutes book (18l7-l874), a list of contributions (1873-1878), a list of membership by sex (n.d.), a list of pastors (1831-1893) and a trustee's annual meeting minute book (1832-1900).

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Congregational Church of West Groton records, #6070. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: West Groton (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.

Congregational churches -- New York (State) -- West Groton.

did.unittitle: "Book of Records for the (WIST) Church," Meeting Minute Book,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The minute book contains a list of the first members, Articles of Faith and Church Covenant, reports from church meetings from neighboring towns, and decisions on membership (admitting and dismissing members). The monthly meeting minutes also include the election of church delegates and deacons and fund raising for the construction of a church building. At the end of the book there is a list of contributions from 1873 until 1878 and a list of membership by sex. Items to note:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A complaint was made charging a member of the church with slander.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A special meeting was held to hear the testimony of a church member who had been accused of leaving the congregational church and meeting with the "Christians." His argument was about his faith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man was excommunicated "for using unduly spirituous liquor, falsifying the truth," and speaking with profane language.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The minute book records the elections of trustees, the question of trustee authority, financial matters, and the erection of a church building. Items of note:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussing centered around the erection of the church building. The church called itself "The Congregational Christ Society of Groton and Lansing."
